formula: Pump Price = (Crude Oil ÷ 42 gallons) + Refining + Distribution + Federal Tax + State Tax + Retail Margin

preWar.label: Before War (Feb 27)
preWar.note: Actual retail average was $3.18 (includes regional variation)

current.label: Current (Mar 18)
current.note: National average at $3.981/gal (AAA March 26, Day 27). Up 25.2% from pre-war $3.18. Just $0.019 from $4.00 milestone. California: $5.84/gal. Diesel ~$5.29/gal. Brent at ~$97.00, WTI $91.64 after Iran rejects 15-point peace plan (down from $99.75 Day 26 close on Iran rejection volatility). Philippines declared national energy emergency. Iran selective Hormuz policy allows 'non-hostile' ships — but only ~6-10 transits/day.

context: AAA national average at $3.981/gallon on March 26 (Day 27), up 25.2% from the $3.18 pre-war baseline. Just $0.019 from the $4.00 milestone — first time since 2022. Diesel at ~$5.29/gallon. California: $5.84/gallon. Brent spiked to $105.85 after Iran definitively rejected the US 15-point peace plan and IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri was killed. Trump Cabinet meeting: Iran 'begging to make a deal.' Hormuz blockade Day 24 — fewer than 10 transits/day vs 138 pre-war. Iran allowing 8-10 tanker passages as 'goodwill gesture.' Pump prices lag oil by 2-3 days — $4.00/gal breach now likely if Brent holds above $105.
